content.problemStatement:
A new patisserie has opened up to rave reviews. You, in your quest for deliciousness, are going to visit it.

The patisserie has NNN pastries. With your trained eye, you judge that the iii-th of them has deliciousness AiA_iAi​.

Of course, you want to eat pastries whose total deliciousness is as high as possible. Unfortunately, you can't just buy everything out.

There are KKK customers in the store, including you. They form a queue to order pastries, of which you're the LLL-th person.

Each customer, including you, will do the following:


Among the remaining pastries, buy the one with the **highest** deliciousness
Then, move to the back of the queue
This will repeat till all the pastries are sold out.

What's the total deliciousness of the pastries you buy?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK, and LLL — the number of pastries, the number of people, and your initial position in the queue.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the deliciousness of the pastries.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the total deliciousness of the pastries you buy.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains three integers N, K and L — the number of pastries, the number of customers in the queue and your initial position (1‑indexed). The second line contains N integers A1 … AN, the deliciousness of each pastry.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total deliciousness of the pastries you will buy.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we sort the pastries descending and sum the values at positions L, L+K, L+2K, … (1‑indexed). The computed sums match the given outputs.
Sorted descending: 5 4 1. You are second in the queue, so you receive the 2nd pastry (value 4).
Sorted descending: 60 50 40 30 20 10. Positions you get: 3rd, 7th (doesn't exist). Only 40 is taken.

solution.approach:
Because every customer always picks the currently most delicious pastry, the sequence of purchased pastries is simply the list of deliciousness values sorted in non‑increasing order. The queue rotates every K purchases, so you receive the pastries whose positions in this sorted list are L, L+K, L+2K, … (1‑indexed). Therefore, for each test case we:
1. Sort the array A in descending order.
2. Iterate over indices i = L‑1, L‑1+K, L‑1+2K, … while i < N and accumulate A[i].
The sum fits in 64‑bit signed integer.
This algorithm is optimal: sorting is O(N log N) and the subsequent scan is O(N/K) ≤ O(N).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case (dominated by sorting). The total over all test cases is O((ΣN) log N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the array of deliciousness values.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it)); L = int(next(it))
        a = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        a.sort(reverse=True)
        ans = sum(a[i] for i in range(L-1, N, K))
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
